stories.story: Kanye West, a hip-hop star, has settled a lawsuit with Daniel Ramos, a paparazzi photographer, with the two shaking hands after an apology. The case, filed by Daniel Ramos and his lawyer Gloria Allred, was set for trial next week but was dismissed "because the case was settled to the satisfaction of the parties." The details of the settlement were not disclosed, but one important aspect was an apology by Kanye West to Daniel Ramos.
key_facts: Kanye West settled lawsuit with Daniel Ramos
Daniel Ramos is a paparazzi photographer
Gloria Allred is Daniel Ramos' lawyer
Case dismissed due to settlement
Settlement details not disclosed
Kanye West apologized to Daniel Ramos
West and Ramos shook hands
shared_context: Kanye West, a hip-hop star, has settled a lawsuit with Daniel Ramos, a paparazzi photographer.
The details of the settlement were not disclosed.

stories.story: Daniel Ramos, a paparazzi photographer, filed a civil suit against Kanye West, a hip-hop star, after West attacked him and tried to wrestle his camera from him in July 2013 at Los Angeles International Airport. Daniel Ramos and his lawyer, Gloria Allred, sought general and punitive damages in the civil suit, saying that Kanye West had interfered with the photographer's rights to pursue a lawful occupation. The case was set for trial next week but was dismissed "because the case was settled to the satisfaction of the parties," though the details of the settlement were not disclosed.
key_facts: Daniel Ramos filed civil suit against Kanye West
West attacked Ramos and tried to wrestle camera in July 2013 at Los Angeles International Airport
Ramos and Allred sought general and punitive damages
Claimed West interfered with lawful occupation
Case set for trial, then dismissed
Settlement details not disclosed
shared_context: Daniel Ramos, a paparazzi photographer, filed a civil suit against Kanye West, a hip-hop star.
The details of the settlement were not disclosed.

stories.story: Kanye West, a hip-hop star, pleaded no contest last year to a misdemeanor count of battery over a scuffle where he attacked Daniel Ramos, a paparazzi photographer, and tried to wrestle his camera from him in July 2013 at Los Angeles International Airport. A judge sentenced Kanye West to two years' probation, as well as anger management sessions and community service. Daniel Ramos and his lawyer, Gloria Allred, also filed a civil suit against Kanye West, which was later settled, though the details of the settlement were not disclosed.
key_facts: Kanye West pleaded no contest to misdemeanor battery
Scuffle involved Daniel Ramos, a paparazzi photographer
Incident in July 2013 at Los Angeles International Airport
West sentenced to two years' probation, anger management, community service
Ramos and Allred filed civil suit
Civil suit settled
Settlement details not disclosed
shared_context: Kanye West, a hip-hop star, pleaded no contest last year to a misdemeanor count of battery over a scuffle where he attacked Daniel Ramos, a paparazzi photographer, in July 2013 at Los Angeles International Airport.
The details of the settlement were not disclosed.

stories.story: The original incident, which led to Kanye West, a hip-hop star, pleading no contest to a misdemeanor count of battery, was caught on video on the night of July 19, 2013, outside a terminal at the Los Angeles airport. In the video, Daniel Ramos, a paparazzi photographer, shouts "Kanye! Kanye! Talk to me, Kanye!" and asks "What's' going on? Why can't we talk to you? I mean, why?" as West moves through a group of paparazzi. As Kanye West advances toward him, Daniel Ramos says "Now come on, Kanye, I don't want to fight with you," to which West replies, "I told you, don't talk to me, right." Kanye West then says, "You're trying to get me in trouble so I step off and have to pay you like $250,000," before rushing Daniel Ramos and attempting to wrestle his camera from his hands, retreating after about 15 seconds of scuffling.

Daniel Ramos (paparazzi photographer) shouted at Kanye West (hip-hop star)

West retreated after 15 seconds
Incident led to West pleading no contest to battery
shared_context: The original incident, which led to Kanye West, a hip-hop star, pleading no contest to a misdemeanor count of battery, was caught on video on the night of July 19, 2013, outside a terminal at the Los Angeles airport.

stories.story: Gloria Allred, lawyer for Daniel Ramos, issued a statement after the settlement of the lawsuit between Daniel Ramos, a paparazzi photographer, and Kanye West, a hip-hop star, saying that "one important aspect of it was an apology by Kanye West to our client, Daniel Ramos." Gloria Allred stated, "We believe that this case sent an important message," adding, "Celebrities are not above the law, and they have no right to physically attack someone simply because they were asked a question." The details of the settlement were not disclosed.
key_facts: Gloria Allred (lawyer for Daniel Ramos) issued statement
Settlement included apology from Kanye West (hip-hop star) to Daniel Ramos (paparazzi photographer)
Allred said case sent important message
Allred stated celebrities are not above the law and have no right to physically attack for questions
Settlement details not disclosed
shared_context: Gloria Allred, lawyer for Daniel Ramos, issued a statement after the settlement of the lawsuit between Daniel Ramos, a paparazzi photographer, and Kanye West, a hip-hop star.
The details of the settlement were not disclosed.

stories.story: In addition to settling a lawsuit with Daniel Ramos, a paparazzi photographer, Kanye West, a hip-hop star, was investigated by Beverly Hills Police for an incident in January 2014. In that incident, Kanye West was accused of assaulting a man at a Beverly Hills chiropractor's office, but he avoided criminal charges by reaching a civil settlement with the man. Separately, Kanye West also apologized to Beck and Bruno Mars. CNN's Sonya Hamasaki contributed to this report.
key_facts: Kanye West (hip-hop star) investigated by Beverly Hills Police January 2014
Accused of assaulting a man at a Beverly Hills chiropractor's office
West avoided criminal charges via civil settlement with the man
West apologized to Beck and Bruno Mars
Sonya Hamasaki (CNN) contributed to report
